steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase "oil painting *Gun with Hand #1*" is a noun phrase modifying the subject of the sentence. The blank separates the noun "oil painting" from its title (*italicized*), while the following dependent clause ('which was created...') requires a punctuation break.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: A comma is needed before the dependent clause ('which was created...') to separate it from the main noun phrase. Option C places a comma after the title to introduce the dependent clause without disrupting the noun-title pairing. The other choices either omit the necessary comma (A, B, D) or misplace punctuation (B: comma before/after title; D: comma immediately after 'painting'). That matches choice C — C is correct.
